title: Making New Homes for Butterflies in Glasgow
summary: In Glasgow, a big project is starting to help butterflies and moths. People are creating 40 new wild spaces in parks, schools, and community places. This is because butterflies and moths are becoming fewer in the UK, and the project wants to stop this. A charity called Butterfly Conservation is working with Glasgow City Council to do this. They will plant wildflowers and make habitats where butterflies can live. Volunteers will help take care of these areas. The project costs £250,000 and is paid for by The National Lottery Heritage Fund and others. Kids in schools can join in too, and there are start-up packs with seeds for people to grow their own mini gardens. This helps nature and makes Glasgow a better place for everyone.

background_read: Butterflies are insects that need flowers and plants to live. In cities, there are fewer green spaces, so butterflies have trouble finding homes. Projects like this one in Glasgow create special areas with wildflowers to attract butterflies and help them survive. This is important because butterflies are part of nature and make our world beautiful. People can help by planting flowers in their gardens or joining community projects.
Article_Structure: WHO: Butterfly Conservation charity and Glasgow City Council
WHAT: Creating 40 wild spaces for butterflies and moths
WHERE: In Glasgow, in parks, schools, and community hubs
WHY: To fight the decline of butterflies and inspire people to help wildlife

perspectives.description: This project helps protect butterflies and improve nature in the city.
